stack: rust / cedar-policy 4 / axum 0.8 / redis 1 / Ory (Hydra/Kratos) reference

research_evidence.finding: MCP authorization follows OAuth 2.1; per-user/agent access control, audit of who-did-what, and per-user rate/usage tracking are first-class concerns — flint-gate's remit.

Independently describes flint-gate's EXISTING design: externalized policy engine queried per tool-call, list_tools filtered by policy decision, decision logs, and 'attenuated permissions derived from the calling user' (the delegation case). Validates embedded Cedar as the equivalent of an external PDP — do NOT adopt Cerbos/OPA. The gap the industry sources do NOT solve — per-agent token/cost budgets — is flint-gate's G2 differentiator.

Ory Hydra supports custom access-token claims via the token/consent hook — the 'Hydra-side claim mapper' option for delegate-classification is real, but rejected as the DEFAULT (fragile per-deployment; only covers the Hydra path, not any-JWKS-IdM).

build_required.rationale: Tool-authz engine + list_tools filtering + audit already exist (validated by the Cerbos reference architecture). DECIDED: classify a token as Agent GATEWAY-SIDE from its RFC 8693 `act` claim at verification time (not a Hydra claim mapper), so delegated agents are governed by agent tool-policy + budget without the gateway rewriting tokens. Honors federate-never-an-IdP and covers any JWKS IdM; Hydra claim mapper documented as an optional operator enhancement.
build_required.open_question: Confirm act/flint_kind precedence for Agent classification in Spec.

build_required.gap: G2 Agent budget scope + fail-closed posture
build_required.rationale: Add BudgetScope::Agent (User|Team today) + key derivation, tied to build-001 classification so delegated agents are budgeted. DECIDED: reuse BackendUnavailablePosture (built last phase); default Agent -> Deny (fail-closed) on a budget-backend outage, User/Team keep the current degrade. Closes the fail-OPEN resolve_budget_usage seam for agent budgets.
build_required.reuses: BudgetScope enum, RedisRateLimiter::incr_budget/get_budget, BackendUnavailablePosture, budget_exceeded() pipeline enforcement

build_required.rationale: record_authz_decision writes only the DB audit trail. Add flint_tool_authz_total{decision} + budget-consumption on the existing admin /metrics, reusing the record_delegate pattern. DECIDED: label by `decision` only (bounded), NOT raw tool name (runtime/attacker-influenced -> cardinality explosion); tool name stays in the DB audit.
Strict cross-replica rate-limit mode (token-endpoint deny posture) + a symmetric metric for the local-mint exchange path. Only if G1-G3 land with budget.

ergonomics_scope: out-of-scope this phase (admin Policies tab + Cedar write-time validation already exist; defer higher-level agent-tool affordance)
